title: Peppery Nutmeg Turkish Cod Stir‑fry
description: A bright, pepper‑and‑nutmeg‑infused cod dish with a splash of Turkish flavor, perfect for a quick kid‑friendly dinner.
instructions: Set a large non‑stick skillet over medium heat and add 1 tbsp olive oil.
Slice the cod into bite‑size cubes, season lightly with salt and ½ tsp pepper.
Add the cod to the skillet, cook for 3 min, then flip and cook another 2 min until just opaque.
Remove cod and set aside; add remaining 1 tbsp oil to the skillet.
Sauté the sumac, dill, capers, parsley, coriander, and a splash of lemon juice for 30 sec until fragrant.
Return the cod to the pan, pour in the honey, then sprinkle nutmeg and remaining pepper, tossing gently to coat.
Cook for an additional 2 min, allowing flavors to meld and the cod to finish cooking.
Remove from heat, drizzle with the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil, and toast sesame seeds in a dry skillet for 1 min before sprinkling on top.
Serve hot with a side of steamed vegetables or a light salad.
